news_headlines.title: Niagen Bioscience Announces First Cruise Ship Clinic Partnership with OneSpaWorld, Bringing the NAD-Boosting Benefits of Niagen IV to the High Seas
news_headlines.summary: Niagen Bioscience has partnered with OneSpaWorld to offer its pharmaceutical-grade Niagen IV in over 80 Medi-Spa clinics on high-end cruise ships. This collaboration marks Niagen Bioscience's first venture into cruise ship clinics, expanding its Niagen Plus footprint and making its NAD-boosting IV available to travelers seeking wellness experiences at sea. The partnership aims to provide a premium, science-backed solution for healthy aging to a new consumer base.

news_headlines.title: Cruise spa operator brings Niagen IV to more than 80 ship clinics
news_headlines.summary: Niagen Bioscience has partnered with OneSpaWorld to offer its pharmaceutical-grade Niagen IV at over 80 Medi-Spa clinics on high-end cruise ships. This collaboration marks Niagen Bioscience's first cruise ship clinic partnership, expanding its Niagen Plus™ footprint globally. The Niagen IV, a next-generation formulation of patented nicotinamide riboside, offers a better patient experience, shorter infusion times, and a faster rise in NAD+ blood levels compared to traditional NAD+ IV, aiming to cater to an increasing demand for science-backed wellness experiences among travelers.

news_headlines.title: Carnival Corporation and Bermuda Leaders Chart a Course for Guest Experience
news_headlines.summary: Carnival Corporation and Bermuda's business and tourism leaders met to discuss enhancing guest experiences and deepening their partnership. Discussions covered expanding shore excursions, ongoing port developments at King’s Wharf, and Bermuda's wider tourism vision. Both parties emphasized a shared belief that the best guest experiences are built collaboratively.

news_headlines.title: Carnival Corporation reports no ‘significant’ cancellation trends
news_headlines.summary: Carnival Corporation has not observed any significant cancellation trends among its cruise lines following the outbreak of war in the Middle East. The company reported record revenues of $6.2 billion for its first quarter and expressed satisfaction with current booking volumes, though they acknowledge the impact of the conflict. Carnival's CEO emphasized the importance of fuel efficiency and the critical role of travel agents in their business model.

news_headlines.title: Extra Space Storage’s Operating Leverage Is Kicking In: Here Is What the $189 Bull Case Requires
news_headlines.summary: Extra Space Storage (EXR) is showing signs of operating leverage kicking in, with its first positive same-store revenue quarter in over a year. The company's Q4 core FFO beat analyst expectations, and new customer move-in rates have turned positive in most top markets, indicating future rent growth. TIKR's valuation model suggests a mid-case price of $189.70 by December 2030, but this depends on continued acceleration of move-in rates and no significant expansion of pricing restrictions.

news_headlines.title: KeyBanc Reiterates Sector Weight Rating on Commercial Metals Company (CMC)
news_headlines.summary: KeyBanc analyst Samuel McKinney reiterated a Sector Weight rating for Commercial Metals Company (CMC). The analyst noted that while FY26 EPS is expected to fall due to M&A accounting, EBITDA remains steady, with precast contributions starting strong. Investor macro concerns and rising U.S. rebar supply might be affecting share performance, but KeyBanc maintains its rating due to FCFE strength, diversification efforts, and valuation support.

news_headlines.title: Carnival Corporation Reports Record Q1 Results as Demand and Bookings Surge
news_headlines.summary: Carnival Corporation reported record-breaking first-quarter results for 2026, with revenues reaching approximately $6.2 billion and adjusted earnings per share increasing by 50% year-on-year. The company's strong performance was driven by surging demand, high yields, and robust booking volumes, leading to a significant turnaround from a loss in the previous year. Carnival also unveiled a new long-term strategy, "PROPEL," to accelerate earnings growth and strengthen shareholder returns, while upgrading its full-year outlook despite acknowledging challenges like rising fuel costs.

news_headlines.title: Norwegian Cruise Line Fell 25% in the Last 30 Days. Here’s Where Shares Could Go in 2026
news_headlines.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) stock dropped 25% in the last 30 days due to weaker 2026 guidance, execution issues, and concerns about consistent profitability despite strong travel demand. The company reported mixed Q4 results and analysts issued cautious updates, lowering price targets. While appearing modestly undervalued with a target price of $25 and an implied upside of 34%, NCLH's future performance hinges on operational improvements and margin recovery to close the gap with competitors like Royal Caribbean.

news_headlines.title: Unity Software StockIs Down 56% in 2026: Do Analysts See Recovery?
news_headlines.summary: Unity Software, a videogame engine maker shifting towards AI-powered advertising, has shown a significant Q1 2026 revenue and EBITDA beat, primarily driven by its AI-driven mobile ad platform, Vector. Despite a 56% stock drop in 2026, Wall Street analysts and TIKR's valuation model see substantial upside, predicting continued margin expansion and growth as Unity divests legacy ad networks and integrates new AI capabilities. The company's future hinges on the successful integration of runtime engine behavioral data into Vector and sustaining its Strategic Grow revenue momentum.
